primaryDescription: Nathan Birch is a professor at BYU-Idaho who helps students build practical web and software development skills.
workDescription1: He is a full-stack web developer and mobile application developer.
workDescription2: He teaches programming, web development, and application design with a focus on real-world projects.
linkTitleText: Check out his professional links below.

primaryDescription: He is a student at BYU Pathway Worldwide.

primaryDescription: Jane Namukasa is a frontend developer who creates responsive interfaces with accessible and maintainable code.

primaryDescription: John Smith is a backend developer interested in reliable APIs, data validation, and service integrations.
workDescription1: He develops REST endpoints, validates request payloads, and documents API behavior.
workDescription2: He has experience connecting applications to cloud databases and external services.
contactText: Contact John at johnith@outlook.com for backend development support.

primaryDescription: Sarah Johnson is a UI/UX designer with a passion for creating intuitive and visually appealing user interfaces.

primaryDescription: Michael Chen is a DevOps engineer focused on CI/CD pipelines, containerization, and cloud infrastructure.

contactText: Reach Michael at michael.chen@email.com for DevOps consulting.

primaryDescription: David Kim is a data analyst who transforms raw data into actionable insights using Python and SQL.

primaryDescription: Olivia Martinez is a cybersecurity analyst dedicated to protecting systems and networks from threats.
